title_original: Draft quantum order tasks many agencies with reinvigorating the tech’s development - Nextgov/FCW
summary_original: A draft executive order from the White House, titled “Ushering In The Next Frontier Of Quantum Innovation,” outlines a coordinated government effort to advance quantum information sciences in 2026. The Office of Science and Technology Policy, along with leaders from the Departments of Energy, Defense, Commerce, and intelligence, will be responsible for updating the National Quantum Strategy within 180 days. The order establishes a national push for a quantum computer for scientific discovery, with at least one system to be housed at a Department of Energy facility and accelerated through private sector partnerships. Other key initiatives include forming a Center of Excellence to evaluate quantum technologies and prioritizing quantum sensing, networking, workforce development, and international trade cooperation.
